R-native agent layer

Source: vignettes/r-native-agent-layer.Rmd
r-native-agent-layer.Rmd

RbebelM’s low-level API exposes model loading, ChatML turns, events, and tool orchestration. The R-native agent layer builds a small layer on top of those pieces: one session object can be driven from the R console loop, from a small JSON-RPC SDK helper, or wrapped in a generic bebelAgentLoop endpoint for the standalone rbebelm-tui frontend.

The layer is intentionally separate from the core model bindings. The hot path is still BebeLM/Rust inference; the R layer owns session policy, tool metadata, console interaction, optional RPC helpers, and external TUI clients.

Create a session 

library(Rbebelm)

model <- bebel_model_load(Sys.getenv("BEBELM_WEIGHTS_FILE"), num_threads = 2)
agent <- bebel_r_agent(
  model,
  allow_eval = TRUE,
  greedy = TRUE,
  max_gen = 128,
  max_think = 16
)
agent

The default agent tool set includes R object/documentation inspection, file tools, and model-callable r_eval/r_plot. Use allow_eval = FALSE, TUI --no-eval, or /no-eval if you want a read-only model tool catalog.

library(Rbebelm)
tools <- bebel_default_r_tools(allow_eval = TRUE)
bebel_agent_tool_catalog(tools)
#>         name
#> 1  r_objects
#> 2     r_eval
#> 3     r_plot
#> 4     r_help
#> 5 list_files
#> 6  read_file
#> 7 grep_files
#>                                                                description
#> 1                            List objects in the configured R environment.
#> 2 Evaluate R code in the configured environment and return printed output.
#> 3          Render R plotting code to a PNG file and return the saved path.
#> 4                        Read R help for a topic, optionally in a package.
#> 5   Fuzzy-search files under a directory using the native FFF file finder.
#> 6                                                        Read a text file.
#> 7                                              Search text files by regex.

Run a turn 

turn <- bebel_r_agent_turn(
  agent,
  "Reply exactly: R agent ready.",
  max_steps = 1,
  on_event = NULL
)
trimws(sub("(?s)^.*</think>\\s*", "", turn$text, perl = TRUE))

For interactive use, start the console loop:

bebel_r_agent_console(agent)

Or use the one-call launcher, which loads the model and then enters the console:

bebel_r_agent_start(Sys.getenv("BEBELM_WEIGHTS_FILE", "LFM2.5-8B-A1B-Q4_K_M.gguf"))

The package also installs a small helper script in inst/bin:

agent_bin <- system.file("bin/rbebelm-agent", package = "Rbebelm")
system2(agent_bin, "--help")

Console commands include /tools, /graphics, /r, /rplot, /transcript, /clear, and /quit. The /r command evaluates R directly in the same environment used by the agent’s R tools. For example, /r x <- mtcars creates an object that r_objects() can later see. Visible /r output is capped so large objects do not flood the chat prompt; assign objects or use summaries such as /r str(x) for inspection. Use /graphics to inspect or change how plots are handled and /rplot plot(mpg ~ cyl, mtcars) to draw. Interactive R consoles use native R graphics by default; headless sessions use PNG artifacts; optional devout-ascii can render terminal text plots when the devout package is installed. The r_eval and r_plot tools are advertised to the model by default for bebel_r_agent(); disable with allow_eval = FALSE or /no-eval when you want read-only model tools. The console prints a compact token/timing stats line after each user turn.

JSON-RPC SDK surface

The same session object can be served over a small JSON-RPC API. This is not an OpenAI-compatible API; it is an SDK surface for controlling the R-native agent. The server uses optional nanonext; JSON parsing and serialization use imported yyjsonr.

server <- bebel_r_agent_rpc_server(agent, url = "http://127.0.0.1:8080")
server$start()
# ... handle requests ...
server$close()

Supported methods:

  • session/info
  • tools/list
  • session/transcript
  • session/clear
  • turn

Example request body for POST /rpc:

{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"turn","params":{"prompt":"List objects in the R session."}}

The console, this RPC helper, and the generic loop endpoint share the same R session, transcript, tool catalog, and BebeLM agent state. The TUI does not duplicate agent logic; it talks to the generic loop endpoint (GET /stream, POST /command, plus /rpc compatibility) built from the R-native session.

On this page
